title: Other registered nursing professionals
description: Plan, direct, or coordinate the operations of public or private sector organizations, overseeing multiple departments or locations. Duties and responsibilities include formulating policies, managing daily operations, and planning the use of materials and human resources, but are too diverse and general in nature to be classified in any one functional area of management or administration, such as personnel, purchasing, or administrative services. Usually manage through subordinate supervisors. Excludes First-Line Supervisors. Plan, direct, or coordinate medical and health services in hospitals, clinics, managed care organizations, public health agencies, or similar organizations. Assist other social and human service providers in providing client services in a wide variety of fields, such as psychology, rehabilitation, or social work, including support for families. May assist clients in identifying and obtaining available benefits and social and community services. May assist social workers with developing, organizing, and conducting programs to prevent and resolve problems relevant to substance abuse, human relationships, rehabilitation, or dependent care. (Based on 16 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Provide medical treatment and personal care to patients, including assisting with bathing, dressing, personal hygiene, and maintaining comfort while also instructing family members in simple nursing tasks.
Collect samples, such as blood and urine, from patients, perform routine laboratory tests on samples, and also collect specimens for laboratory tests as needed.
Measure and record patients' vital signs and provide basic patient care or treatments, such as taking temperatures or blood pressures and dressing wounds.
Answer patients' calls, determine how to assist them, and evaluate nursing intervention outcomes in conjunction with other healthcare team members.
Administer prescribed medications, including intravenous fluids, and monitor patients for reactions and side effects while noting times and amounts on patient charts.
Observe and assess patients' conditions, charting and reporting changes, and taking necessary actions including monitoring for signs of complications.
Supervise nursing aides or assistants and coordinate patient care conferences to discuss care and treatment plans.
Work as part of a healthcare team to assess patient needs, plan and modify care accordingly, and implement appropriate interventions.
Document patients' medical histories, assessment findings, treatment plans, interventions, outcomes, and any plan revisions for continuity of care.
Assist clients in obtaining available benefits and social and community services, collaborating with them to plan for future health care needs.
Assess patients' psychosocial status, pain levels, family adaptation levels, and health conditions to provide appropriate interventions.
Coordinate and participate in health care quality initiatives, including preparing reports to inform management about the status and implementation of programs.
Develop and implement organizational policies and procedures, and manage personnel operations including recruitment, hiring, training, and supervision.
Participate in the development of nursing practice protocols, educate staff members, and lead compliance with regulatory processes.
Evaluate the impact of illnesses on patients' health and function and monitor changes in status and indications of conditions via appropriate interventions.
Provide emotional support for patients and their families, advocating for their needs and ensuring they are cared for respectfully and compassionately.
Monitor and document the use of diagnostic services, staff resources, and develop or expand medical programs or health services.
Participate in professional development activities to improve nursing knowledge and skills by reading current literature and engaging with professional organizations.
Perform clerical tasks, including making appointments, keeping patient records, and possibly sterilizing equipment and supplies as required.
Provide personal care for deceased individuals, including washing and dressing bodies as part of post-mortem care and related activities.
Ensure proper storage and cleaning of medical equipment after use to maintain safety and sanitation standards.
Adjust patient assistive devices and monitor patients for their reactions to treatments or interventions as necessary.
Communicate with patients and their families to discuss health issues, treatment options, and provide health education and counseling.
Read current literature, talk with colleagues, or participate in professional organizations or conferences to keep abreast of developments in nursing.

Instruct nurses, residents, interns, students, or other staff on topics such as anesthetic techniques, pain management, and emergency responses, as well as assess training needs or conduct training sessions for nursing students or medical staff.

Educate patients about self-management of acute or chronic illnesses, tailoring instructions to patients' individual circumstances, and counsel patients about drug regimens and possible side effects or interactions with other substances.
Diagnose or treat acute and chronic health care problems, and monitor or evaluate medical conditions of patients in collaboration with other health care professionals.

Analyze and interpret patients' histories, symptoms, physical findings, or diagnostic information to develop appropriate diagnoses and treatment plans, including ordering, performing, or interpreting results of diagnostic tests.
Prepare patients for and assist with examinations or treatments and monitor all aspects of patient care, including diet and physical activity.
Provide direct care by performing comprehensive health assessments, developing differential diagnoses, and administering medications as required.

Consult and coordinate with healthcare team members to assess, plan, implement, or evaluate patient care plans, and provide emotional support to patients and families in areas such as caring for infants or adapting to disability.

Develop, implement, or evaluate standards of nursing practice in specialty areas, and assess needs of individuals, families, or communities, including home or work environment assessments, to identify health or safety problems.
Maintain accurate, detailed reports and records of patient care, condition, progress, or any issues to report and discuss with supervisors or case managers.
Monitor, record, and report symptoms or changes in patients' conditions and evaluate patients' behavior to formulate diagnoses or assess treatments.

Conduct individual, group, or family psychotherapy for those with chronic or acute mental disorders and educate patients and family members about mental health and medical conditions, including preventive measures.
Provide clients with communication assistance, transport clients to appointments, and engage in research activities related to nursing.
Supervise nursing care staff and engage in case reviews, consulting with the team caring for the client to evaluate needs and plan for continuing services.
Assess clients' cognitive abilities and physical and emotional needs to determine appropriate interventions, and develop and implement behavioral management and care plans for clients.

Participate in activities aimed at professional growth and development, including conferences or continuing education activities; maintain departmental policies, procedures, objectives, or infection control standards.
Refer patients to specialized health resources or community agencies for assistance, and consult with institutions regarding issues relevant to the practice of nursing.
Perform healthcare-related tasks, such as monitoring vital signs, under the direction of registered nurses or physiotherapists.
Observe nurses and visit patients to ensure proper nursing care and maintain departmental infection control standards.
Train workers in proper operational procedures and functions and explain company policies.
Arrange worker breaks to ensure services are adequately staffed throughout each shift.
Direct or coordinate the activities of workers, such as hotel staff or hair stylists, and resolve employee disputes or complaints following management rules and regulations.
Meet with managers or other supervisors to stay informed of changes affecting operations and to discuss operational data and activity reports.
Requisition necessary supplies, equipment, or services and inspect materials, stock, vehicles, equipment, or facilities to ensure that they comply with established standards.
Observe and evaluate workers' appearance and performance to ensure quality service and compliance with specifications.
Assign work schedules following work requirements to ensure quality and timely delivery of service.
Participate in continuing education to stay abreast of industry trends and developments.
Resolve customer complaints regarding worker performance or services rendered and apply customer feedback to service improvement efforts.
Enforce safety rules and regulations.
